refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it requires explicit definition of: (1) the measurement period and baseline, (2) what constitutes 'available' vs. 'unavailable' (e.g., does safety shutdown count as unavailability?), (3) which subsystems or failure modes dominate availability loss, and (4) the allocation of availability budget across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services domains. High safety criticality means that safety-driven shutdowns must be distinguished from reliability failures.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Collect operational telemetry (uptime/downtime logs, failure modes, maintenance events) over the agreed evaluation period. Calculate availability as (Total Time - Unplanned Downtime) / Total Time. Stratify by subsystem (propulsion, hydraulics, teleoperation link, safety system) to identify bottlenecks. Compare against customer-defined target and contractual acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: FR-AVAIL-001: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve ≥0.98 availability (propulsion, hydraulics, structural integrity, sensors).
FR-AVAIL-002: The Teleoperation subsystem shall achieve ≥0.99 availability (network link, control latency, operator interface).
FR-AVAIL-003: The Safety Services subsystem shall achieve ≥0.99 availability (safety monitoring, alert generation, emergency shutdown).
FR-AVAIL-004: The NGHE solution shall provide predictive maintenance recommendations to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-AVAIL-005: The NGHE solution shall log and report all availability-impacting events (failures, maintenance, safety shutdowns) for post-evaluation analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundancy and fault tolerance across all critical subsystems (dual sensors, backup hydraulic circuits, network failover) combined with predictive maintenance scheduling based on real-time health monitoring.

DP-B: Establish a modular architecture with hot-swappable components (battery packs, hydraulic modules, teleoperation units) and rapid field replacement procedures to minimize mean-time-to-repair (MTTR).

DP-C: Deploy continuous health monitoring and diagnostics across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; integrate with a centralized availability management system that tracks KPI in real-time and triggers preventive actions.

DP-D: Allocate availability budgets to each subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety) based on failure mode analysis (FMEA); design each subsystem to meet its allocated target; validate through accelerated life testing and field trials.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C + DP-D (integrated approach): Deploy continuous health monitoring and diagnostics across all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) integrated with a centralized availability management system that tracks the KPI in real-time and triggers preventive actions. Simultaneously, allocate availability budgets to each subsystem based on FMEA; design each subsystem to meet its allocated target; validate through accelerated life testing and field trials.

potential_sources: DP-C (monitoring) and DP-D (allocation) are complementary: monitoring provides visibility into whether allocated targets are being met; allocation ensures each subsystem is designed to contribute proportionally to the overall KPI.

Risk: If monitoring system itself becomes a single point of failure, it could mask availability issues. Mitigation: Ensure monitoring system is itself highly available (≥0.99) and has graceful degradation modes.

Risk: Allocation targets may be infeasible for one or more subsystems (e.g., Teleoperation network link availability constrained by ISP). Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study during DP refinement; adjust targets or add redundancy (e.g., dual network providers).
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP avoids over-coupling by separating concerns: DP-C focuses on observability and real-time management; DP-D focuses on design-time allocation and validation. Both are necessary and neither is sufficient alone.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Customer will provide or accept a specific evaluation period (e.g., 12 months of continuous operation) and definition of 'available' vs. 'unavailable' (e.g., safety shutdowns do not count as unavailability if they are planned/predictable).
Assumption: Predictive maintenance and health monitoring data are available and actionable (i.e., sensors, algorithms, and maintenance procedures are mature enough to reduce unplanned downtime).

Risk: Availability is highly dependent on external factors (operator skill, worksite conditions, supply chain for spare parts, network infrastructure). DP-C and DP-D address internal design but cannot fully control external factors.

Risk: High safety criticality means that safety-driven shutdowns may be frequent, reducing measured availability. Clarification needed: should safety shutdowns count as 'unavailability' or be excluded from the KPI calculation?
Risk: Teleoperation network link availability is constrained by ISP/infrastructure; may be difficult to achieve ≥0.99 without redundant network providers or hybrid (tele + autonomous) operation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Before proceeding to IM assignment, conduct a feasibility study to: (1) clarify the definition of 'available' and treatment of safety shutdowns, (2) allocate availability budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services based on preliminary FMEA, (3) identify which subsystems are at risk of not meeting their allocated targets, and (4) propose design changes (redundancy, modular replacement, network failover) to close gaps. This will inform the IM assignment and ensure that the selected modules can realistically achieve the KPI.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating availability across all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). It owns the centralized availability management system, health monitoring and diagnostics platform, real-time KPI tracking, and preventive action triggers. It also owns the allocation of availability budgets to subordinate modules and the validation/verification of the overall KPI.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives health and status data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Used to populate the centralized monitoring system and calculate real-time availability metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends preventive maintenance recommendations and operational directives (e.g., reduce duty cycle, schedule maintenance window) to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems based on health monitoring insights.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety alerts from Safety Services; may trigger availability-impacting actions (e.g., emergency shutdown, mode transition). Also sends availability-related alerts to Safety Services if monitoring system detects degradation that could affect safety.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Uses network infrastructure to communicate with remote monitoring/analytics systems, customer dashboards, and maintenance scheduling systems. Availability of this link is critical to the effectiveness of the monitoring and preventive action system.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of the centralized availability management system, health monitoring platform, and KPI tracking. It has clear ownership of the integration logic and cross-subsystem orchestration. Minimal diffusion: only the interfaces to subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are external.

Vehicle could own its own health monitoring and availability target (FR-AVAIL-001), but the centralized KPI tracking, cross-subsystem orchestration, and preventive action logic should not be contained in Vehicle. High diffusion risk if availability management is split across Vehicle and other modules.

Teleoperation could own its own availability target (FR-AVAIL-002), but the centralized monitoring and KPI tracking should not be contained here. High diffusion risk if availability management is fragmented.

Safety Services owns safety monitoring and alert generation (FR-AVAIL-003), but should not own the overall availability KPI tracking or cross-subsystem orchestration. High diffusion risk if availability management is mixed with safety-specific logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of the availability KPI and the centralized management system that realizes DP-C and DP-D. This module will integrate health monitoring data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; calculate real-time availability metrics; allocate budgets to subordinate modules; and trigger preventive actions. Subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will own their own health monitoring sensors and subsystem-level availability targets, but will report to and be orchestrated by NGHE Solution.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a single point of failure (e.g., centralized monitoring system crashes), the entire availability management system is compromised. Mitigation: Design NGHE Solution with high availability (≥0.99), redundant monitoring nodes, and graceful degradation modes.

Risk: Interfaces between NGHE Solution and subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must be well-defined and stable. If these interfaces are poorly specified, diffusion of availability logic across module boundaries will occur. Mitigation: Clearly specify the Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert interfaces; use formal contracts or APIs.

Risk: The Network Link interface is critical to the effectiveness of the monitoring system. If network availability is poor, the centralized system cannot function. Mitigation: Design for network redundancy (dual ISPs, hybrid tele+autonomous operation); ensure graceful degradation if network is unavailable.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Before finalizing IM assignment, specify the detailed interfaces between NGHE Solution and subordinate modules: (1) Telemetry schema (what health data is collected, at what frequency, from which sensors), (2) Tasking schema (what preventive actions can be triggered, how are they prioritized), (3) SafetyAlert integration (how are safety alerts correlated with availability events), and (4) Network Link redundancy strategy (what happens if primary network is unavailable). Also, define the internal architecture of NGHE Solution: will it be a single monolithic system or a distributed set of microservices? This will affect the feasibility of achieving ≥0.99 availability for the monitoring system itself.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (continuous health monitoring + availability budget allocation) directly addresses the FR (achieve ≥0.97 availability). DP-C provides the observability and real-time management needed to detect and respond to availability threats; DP-D provides the design-time rigor to ensure each subsystem is engineered to contribute to the overall KPI. The combination is necessary and sufficient to realize the FR, subject to the assumptions and risks noted above.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If health monitoring logic is duplicated across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules instead of being centralized in NGHE Solution, diffusion will occur and maintenance burden will increase. Mitigation: Enforce a single source of truth for availability KPI tracking in NGHE Solution; subordinate modules report only their subsystem-level health data.

Risk: If preventive action logic is scattered across multiple modules, coordination and prioritization will be difficult. Mitigation: Centralize preventive action orchestration in NGHE Solution; subordinate modules implement only their assigned actions.
Risk: If the Network Link interface is not reliable, the centralized monitoring system cannot function. Mitigation: Design for network redundancy and graceful degradation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the clear owner of the centralized availability management system. Subordinate modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will own their subsystem-level health monitoring and availability targets, but will be orchestrated by NGHE Solution. This containment strategy minimizes diffusion and provides clear ownership boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR (achieve ≥0.97 availability) is feasible through operational telemetry collection and post-evaluation analysis. The NGHE Solution module will own the KPI tracking and reporting; subordinate modules will provide the health data needed to calculate the KPI. Verification will require an agreed evaluation period (e.g., 12 months) and clear definition of 'available' vs. 'unavailable'.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The FR has been decomposed into sub-FRs for Vehicle (≥0.98), Teleoperation (≥0.99), and Safety Services (≥0.99) availability. These allocations are preliminary and should be validated through FMEA and feasibility study. The NGHE Solution module will own the allocation logic and ensure that subordinate modules meet their targets.

recommended_revisions: Conduct a feasibility study to validate the allocated availability targets for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. If any target is infeasible, adjust the allocation or propose design changes (redundancy, modular replacement, network failover).

Clarify the definition of 'available' and the treatment of safety shutdowns. Should safety shutdowns count as 'unavailability' or be excluded from the KPI calculation? This will significantly impact the measured availability and the design of the system.
Specify the detailed interfaces between NGHE Solution and subordinate modules (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Use formal contracts or APIs to ensure clarity and stability.
Define the internal architecture of NGHE Solution (monolithic vs. distributed). Ensure that the monitoring system itself is highly available (≥0.99) and has graceful degradation modes.
Develop a network redundancy strategy to ensure that the centralized monitoring system can function even if the primary network link is unavailable.

decision.justification: The FR has been successfully refined and the DP has been selected and validated at the FR-DP gate. The IM assignment is clear: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of the centralized availability management system. However, before proceeding to detailed design, the sub-FRs (FR-AVAIL-001 through FR-AVAIL-005) must be decomposed further to ensure that each subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) has clear, achievable availability targets and design requirements. Additionally, the recommended revisions (feasibility study, interface specification, network redundancy strategy) must be completed to de-risk the design and ensure that the allocated targets are realistic.
decision.justification_detail: This is a KPI-level requirement that spans multiple subsystems and depends on external factors (network infrastructure, operator skill, worksite conditions). A single-level decomposition is insufficient to ensure that the design is feasible and that all stakeholders understand their responsibilities. Zigzagging down to the next level will allow us to: (1) allocate availability budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services based on FMEA, (2) identify design changes needed to meet allocated targets, (3) specify detailed interfaces and integration points, and (4) develop verification and validation strategies for each subsystem.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-AVAIL-001: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve ≥0.98 availability (propulsion, hydraulics, structural integrity, sensors).
FR-AVAIL-002: The Teleoperation subsystem shall achieve ≥0.99 availability (network link, control latency, operator interface).
FR-AVAIL-003: The Safety Services subsystem shall achieve ≥0.99 availability (safety monitoring, alert generation, emergency shutdown).
FR-AVAIL-004: The NGHE solution shall provide predictive maintenance recommendations to minimize unplanned downtime.
FR-AVAIL-005: The NGHE solution shall log and report all availability-impacting events (failures, maintenance, safety shutdowns) for post-evaluation analysis.

assumptions: Customer will provide or accept a specific evaluation period (e.g., 12 months of continuous operation) and definition of 'available' vs. 'unavailable'.
Predictive maintenance and health monitoring data are available and actionable (sensors, algorithms, and maintenance procedures are mature).
Network infrastructure is available and can support the centralized monitoring system (or redundant network providers can be arranged).
Operator skill and worksite conditions are within normal ranges and do not significantly degrade availability.
Safety shutdowns are either excluded from the KPI calculation or are infrequent enough not to dominate the measured availability.
open_risks: Availability is highly dependent on external factors (operator skill, worksite conditions, supply chain for spare parts, network infrastructure) that are outside the control of the NGHE design team.
High safety criticality means that safety-driven shutdowns may be frequent, reducing measured availability. Clarification needed on treatment of safety shutdowns in the KPI calculation.
Teleoperation network link availability is constrained by ISP/infrastructure; may be difficult to achieve ≥0.99 without redundant network providers or hybrid (tele + autonomous) operation.
Centralized monitoring system could become a single point of failure if not designed with high availability and graceful degradation.
Allocated availability targets for Vehicle (≥0.98), Teleoperation (≥0.99), and Safety Services (≥0.99) are preliminary and may be infeasible for one or more subsystems.
evidence_needed: FMEA for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems to identify failure modes and their impact on availability.
Feasibility study to validate the allocated availability targets and identify design changes needed to meet them.
Detailed interface specifications (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) between NGHE Solution and subordinate modules.
Network redundancy strategy and cost-benefit analysis for dual ISPs or hybrid tele+autonomous operation.
Accelerated life testing and field trial data to validate that the designed system can achieve the allocated availability targets.
Clarification from customer on the definition of 'available' and the treatment of safety shutdowns in the KPI calculation.
